commit.message:
Claude/split onboarding article 22w sh (#3)

* Split onboarding article into four focused parts

The original single article covering plugins, skills, hooks, and rollout
was too long for engineers to follow in one sitting. Split into:

- Part 1 (2026-02-06): Foundation — the problem + CLAUDE.md
- Part 2 (2026-02-13): Plugins and the Marketplace
- Part 3 (2026-02-20): Skills: Encoding Team Rituals
- Part 4 (2026-02-27): Hooks + Putting It All Together / Rollout plan

Each article links to the others in the series header and footer.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01AVwLvqt4aV5tvSXcmA8FAZ

* Add plain-English callouts before code blocks across all four parts

Each technical code block now has a preceding blockquote that explains
what the configuration does in plain English — written for readers who
don't need to implement it themselves. Engineers can skip the callouts;
PMs and non-technical readers can skip the code blocks.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01AVwLvqt4aV5tvSXcmA8FAZ

* Add accompanying template repo and link from all four articles

template/ contains a complete, ready-to-adapt set of Claude Code team
configuration files: CLAUDE.md, package-level CLAUDE.md for monorepos,
.claude/settings.json with all four hook types, four skills (deploy,
new-endpoint, review, incident), and the format-and-lint /
check-no-secrets hook scripts.

All four articles in the series now link to the template at the top
(in the series nav), bottom (in continue reading), and Part 4 includes
a dedicated callout before the rollout section.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01AVwLvqt4aV5tvSXcmA8FAZ

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

6 tests including XOR (non-linear separability).

commit.message:
fix: dark mode not applying — move dark class to document.documentElement

Tailwind's darkMode: 'class' requires .dark on an ANCESTOR element.
Putting it on the same div as dark: variants means the CSS selector
'.dark .dark:xxx' never matches. Fix: toggle class on <html> via useEffect.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Filter: Typbewusste Operatoren, BETWEEN, IS NULL, Datum-Support

Backend: ColumnMeta mit type-Feld (TEXT/NUMBER/DATE), FilterCondition
mit value2 fuer BETWEEN, FilterQueryBuilder erweitert um >=, <=,
BETWEEN, IS NULL, IS NOT NULL und ISO-Datum-Parsing mit Validierung.
Frontend: Typ-abhaengige Operator-Dropdowns, adaptive Eingabefelder
(datetime-local fuer DATE, Dual-Input fuer BETWEEN, kein Input fuer
IS NULL/IS NOT NULL), angepasste Chips und SQL-Vorschau.

commit.message:
Fix _repl AttributeError: stop sharing tool instances across requests

GENERIC_LOCAL_TOOLS was a module-level singleton list — all concurrent
root agent requests shared the same PythonREPLTool/BashRunTool instances.
When one request's close() deleted _repl, other concurrent requests got
AttributeError.

Replace singleton with _build_generic_tools() that creates fresh instances
per request (same pattern subagents already used via _build_fresh_generic_tools,
now consolidated).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: BPF verifier 1M insn limit on xdp_policy, VRF bind race in cluster

Two fixes:

1. xdp_policy BPF verifier regression: resolve_pkt_app_id() and
   preid_log_flags() were __always_inline, causing instruction explosion
   when inlined into the already-large policy evaluation loop (32-entry
   app_ranges loop × policy rules × SNAT rules). Changed to __noinline
   to keep them in separate call frames.

2. Cluster VRF bind race: when BPF dp.Load() fails (config-only mode),
   applyConfig was only called inside the dp-success branch, so VRFs
   were never created and mgmtVRFInterfaces stayed nil. Heartbeat/sync
   sockets couldn't bind to em0/fab addresses. Fix: moved applyConfig
   outside dp-success block + added fallback VRF detection via
   net.InterfaceByName("vrf-mgmt").

commit.message:
Fix code review issues: correctness, resilience, and modularity

- ChannelSummary: guard tap.message?.content against undefined to prevent
  'Someone shoulder-tapped you about: undefined'
- MessageCard: add optional chaining on getUser() for shoulder-tap avatar
  to prevent crash when user record isn't yet loaded
- localStorage: wrap JSON.parse in try/catch so corrupted storage values
  fall back to the default instead of throwing
- UserSettingsView: include actual file size in photo-too-large error message
- messagePagination: reduce banter expiry interval from 60 s to 30 s so
  messages don't linger up to a minute past their TTL
- MessageBox: use auto-fit emoji grid so it adapts to narrow viewports
- TheToolbar: precompute channel/message result index offsets as computed
  refs instead of re-evaluating inline on every render
- InboxView: split fetchChannelSummaries into three focused functions
  (fetchChannelSummariesById, fetchChannelSummariesInitial,
  fetchChannelSummariesNext) and separate the combined watch into two
  independent watches so the initial fetch and the missing-channels fetch
  can no longer fire in the same tick and corrupt each other's state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add demand-driven offer seller prefetch (top-N best offers per DEX pair)

Replace the reverted all-offers seller prefetch with a targeted approach:
only prefetch seller account + trustline keys for the top-N best offers in
asset pairs referenced by the current ledger's TX set DEX operations.

With N=10 and ~50-200 pairs per ledger this produces ~1500-6000 keys
instead of ~2M from scanning all 911K offers, mirroring stellar-core's
demand-driven populateEntryCacheFromBestOffers.

Changes:
- offer_index: add top_n_offer_keys() returning cheapest N offers per pair
- entries: expose top_n_offer_keys() and get_offer_by_key() on LedgerStateManager
- mod: replace collect_offer_seller_keys() (all offers) with
  collect_seller_keys_for_pairs(pairs, n) (targeted per-pair)
- tx_set: add collect_dex_asset_pairs() to extract DEX pairs from TX ops,
  wire seller prefetch into run_transactions_on_executor after static prefetch
- Remove iter_offers() (was only used by the old all-offers method)

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix bugs across all pages: field mismatches, API response unwrapping, Decimal handling

- useAuth(): fix double-wrapped user object ({user:{...}} treated as user directly)
- Members: fix pagination (data.total → data.pagination.total), unwrap {member} response,
  fix field names (address→residentialAddress, joinDate→dateJoined), fix sections dropdown
- Attendance: fix sections API unwrap, fix redirect after creation (session.id → result.session.id),
  fix handleFinalize to abort on save failure
- Penalties: fix field name (type→penaltyType), fix Decimal handling with Number(), fix pagination,
  support comma-separated status filter in API
- Payments: fix field names (amount→amountPaid, method→paymentMethod), fix form submission to
  match paymentSchema (amountDue/amountPaid/paymentMethod), fix Decimal handling
- Events: fix field name (startTime→time), fix pagination
- Announcements: fix pagination
- Reports: fix field names (memberName→member.firstName/lastName, type→penaltyType,
  method→paymentMethod, totalOutstanding→totalBalance, totalCollected→totalPaid)
- Settings: fix settings save payload (remove wrapper), fix nested button in DialogTrigger
- My Profile: unwrap {member} API response

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: task 1A — Auth Backend (#4)

* feat: implement task 1A — Auth Backend

- Set up dj-rest-auth + django-allauth with token authentication
- POST /api/v1/auth/register/ — creates user, returns auth token
- POST /api/v1/auth/login/ — authenticates user, returns auth token
- POST /api/v1/auth/logout/ — invalidates token (requires auth)
- POST /api/v1/auth/token/refresh/ — rotates current token
- register_user / login_user / logout_user actions in accounts/actions/
- lib/errors.py — domain exception hierarchy (BusinessError subclasses)
- lib/exceptions.py — DRF exception handler mapping domain errors to HTTP
- @extend_schema_view annotations on all views for accurate OpenAPI spec
- 55 tests: unit tests for actions + API contract tests for all endpoints

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: add PR link for task 1A

---------

commit.message:
Add vector-valued VIE-2 and VIDE solvers (D extension only)

- New extern(C) entry points: volterra_solve_vie2_vec, volterra_solve_vide_vec
- Compile-time (d≤8) and LAPACK runtime (d>8) implementations
- Python wrappers: solve_vie2_vec_d, solve_vide_vec_d in _dlang.py
- solve_VIE_2 and solve_VIDE now dispatch to vector path for 3-D kernels
- solve_VIDE accepts soln_init_value as scalar or length-d array in vector mode
- Tests: 4 new vector accuracy tests (VIE-2 diagonal, VIE-2 analytic 2D,
  VIDE diagonal, VIDE analytic 2D); 63/63 pass

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: volterra-equation-solvers
repository.description: Collocation-method solvers for Volterra integral and integro-differential equations.

commit.message:
Add trending subcategories: Popular, Rising, Hidden Gems, New Arrivals

Four category pills in the trending section let users discover different
types of trending feeds beyond just mass-appeal popularity:
- Popular: high absolute subscription velocity (existing behavior)
- Rising: high growth rate relative to subscriber base (min 5 subs)
- Hidden Gems: high read engagement per subscriber (small/mid feeds)
- New Arrivals: feeds created within the last year gaining subscribers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: use full words for child-dev widget age display (#534)

* refactor: split AuthContext into domain hooks

Extract 4 modules from the 657-line AuthContext god object:
- restoreUserData: pure function for profile→localStorage restoration
- useFirestoreSync: 9 auto-sync useEffects (window event→Firestore)
- useCityManager: city CRUD state + callbacks
- usePreferences: preference update callbacks

AuthContext now composes these hooks (~276 lines). Zero consumer
changes — useAuth() API and AuthContextType interface are identical.

Includes 48 unit tests covering all extracted modules.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use full words for child-dev widget age display

Align child-dev widget age format with baby tracker style — use i18n
translated full words (e.g., "19 months old, 15 days") instead of
abbreviations ("19m 15d") for consistency across MFEs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
[Agent] feat(PVUI): Platform-specific keyboard layouts: C64, ZX Spectrum, Amstrad CPC (#2595)

* feat(PVUI): platform-specific virtual keyboard layouts (C64, ZX Spectrum, Amstrad CPC)

Add VirtualKeyboardLayouts.swift and VirtualKeyboardView.swift to
PVUI/Sources/PVUIBase/SwiftUI/VirtualKeyboard/ implementing:

- VirtualKeyboardLayout enum: full, compact, functionRow, c64, zxSpectrum, amstradCPC
- C64 layout: C= (LWIN), RUN/STOP (ESC proxy), RESTORE (PrintScreen/NMI),
  CLR/HOME, INST/DEL, CRSR keys, F1-F8, historically-correct rows
- ZX Spectrum layout: CAPS SHIFT and SYMBOL SHIFT as sticky modifiers,
  BREAK (Pause proxy), convenience arrow row
- Amstrad CPC layout: CPC function keys f0-f9, CLR, CPY, DEL, numeric keypad
- VirtualKeyboardView: SwiftUI overlay with layout picker toolbar, sticky
  modifier support, press/release delegate callbacks
- VirtualKeyboardViewModel: @Published layout and activeModifiers state
- VirtualKeyboardDelegate protocol for keyDown/keyUp events

Part of #2585

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: correct invalid GCKeyCode names in VirtualKeyboardLayouts

- Replace .leftBracket/.rightBracket with .openBracket/.closeBracket
  (GCKeyCode uses USB HID names; leftBracket/rightBracket don't exist)
- Replace .backslash proxy for C64 pound key with .scrollLock per spec
- Replace duplicate .equalSign for C64 ASDF = key with .nonUSPound
  (avoids collision with .equalSign used for + on the number row)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address AI review — remove dead || true, fix copyright year

- Remove always-true clause from VirtualKeyboardOverlayModifier,
  restoring unconditional rendering (opacity controls visibility)
- Update copyright year from 2024 to 2026

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Initial release of arch_test v0.1.0

ArchUnit-inspired architecture testing library for Elixir. Enforces
dependency rules, layered architecture, bounded-context isolation,
naming conventions, coupling metrics, and code conventions — all from
compiled BEAM bytecode via :xref, no source parsing required.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
